reason: UNSTATED JUSTIFICATION: p^־± IS A FOUR-VECTOR ג€” The proof lists 'p^־±(ֿ„): a four-vector' without justification. While this is standard physics, a rigorous proof should state the chain: (1) ־³^־±(ֿ„) transforms as x'^־± = ־›^־±_־² ־³^־²(ֿ„) by definition of the transformed worldline; (2) ֿ„ is a Lorentz scalar (proper time is frame-independent); (3) therefore ־³ּ‡^־± = d־³^־±/dֿ„ transforms as ־³ּ‡'^־± = ־›^־±_־² ־³ּ‡^־² (derivative of a four-vector by an invariant parameter is a four-vector); (4) p^־± = m ־³ּ‡^־± with m a scalar, so p^־± transforms as p'^־± = ־›^־±_־² p^־². Similarly for ־³ּ‡^־². The proof should at minimum reference the definition p^־± = m ־³ּ‡^־± from line 57 of lecA1.tex. This is a minor gap since the facts are standard, but a proof that claims 'rigour' (as this addendum does ג€” see lines 23-31 of lecA1.tex, 'we supply the missing rigour') should not leave standard transformations unjustified.
